purpose: Proof MCP initialization seed for cluster-c-restructure-understand. All rules invalidated per designer (master plan, A, B.1, B.2, all cluster C drafts). Problem statement and concerns approved. Evidence pruned to load-bearing facts only. Use this to populate the proof MCP after session restart.

session_context.rules_invalidation_scope: master plan R1-R10, cluster A rules, cluster B.1 R1-R9 + RC-1-5 + NCON-1-3, cluster B.2 RC1-7 + NCON-1-7 + S-RULEs, all cluster C drafts (R03, R13, R20, R24-R39)
session_pivots: two-system architecture questioned and replaced by one-system (proof MCP from session start, no Phase 4a/4b separation)
Plan Mode confirmed failed (did nothing, caused transition friction)
subagent dispatch infeasible during conversation (>5min/round breaks feasibility)
designer-codebase asymmetry recognized (250k-LOC systems mean designer can't be authority on patterns)
channel-don't-prohibit principle is foundational from plan-mode-design-guard

problem_statement: Redesign design-large-task as a one-system architecture using the existing proof MCP throughout, eliminating the Phase 4a / Phase 4b separation while preserving the original Chester insight of channeling completion drive into structured artifacts. Address: how the proof initializes from invocation + research, how information advances the proof under designer consent, how the conversation discipline channels without the failed in-conversation gating mechanisms, how the resulting proof transfers to design-specify with sufficient fidelity to produce architecture matching designer intent.

concerns.label: Proof system transition into design system
concerns.description: How to transition the design proof system into the design system (one-system architecture migration)

concerns.label: Interview initiation to set the proof
concerns.description: Initiation of the interview to initially set the proof

concerns.label: Information presentation to advance the proof
concerns.description: How information is presented to the designer to properly advance the proof

concerns.label: Proof advancement requires designer consent
concerns.description: Agent advancing the proof without consent of the designer

concerns.label: Draft rule re-evaluation under new architecture
concerns.description: All of the draft rules — re-evaluate which apply, which dissolve, which need revising under one-system model

concerns.label: Balance between proof and interview responsibilities
concerns.description: Balancing what the proof does versus what the interview does

concerns.label: Specify handoff fidelity to designer intent
concerns.description: Proper information provided to design-specify, needed to create proper architecture that correctly addresses designer's intent

evidence.statement: design-large-task skill currently exists with two-stage Phase 4a / Phase 4b structure
Proof MCP exists with element types: NC, Rule, Permission, Evidence, Risk, Concern, Resolve Condition, Friction
Subagent dispatch during conversation is infeasible at >5 min/round
Designer-codebase asymmetry: designer cannot be authority on patterns in 250k-LOC systems
Five prior interview systems failed (chester small-task / generic LLM-as-interviewer; classic; architectural; problemfocused; team-interview; lens architecture rebuild)

Channel-don't-prohibit principle is foundational from plan-mode-design-guard rationale: agent's completion drive needs productive redirection via structured artifact production, not behavioral prohibition

next_steps_for_resumed_session: Read this seed file at session start
Initialize proof MCP with problem_statement above
Add concerns CN-1 through CN-7 via manage_concerns
Add evidence EV-A, EV-B, EV-D, EV-E, EV-F, EV-G via submit_proof_update
Begin per-turn solve cycle: agent proposes NCs/Rules/Permissions/Risks/RCs anchored to concerns; designer ratifies each; no element advances without consent (CN-4)
Closure via two-yes gate: designer reviews closing argument and says go or back
